pattern: Because deletions only collapse runs of mutually anagram neighbours, one pass keeping a word only when it differs from the last kept word produces the final array.
hints: Two adjacent words are anagrams exactly when their sorted letters match. What survives a run of such words?
Repeatedly deleting the second of any adjacent anagram pair is the same as dropping every word equal (as a letter multiset) to the previous kept word.
Walk left to right; keep a word only if its sorted form differs from the sorted form of the previously kept word. The first word is always kept.

edge_inputs.description: runs of anagrams collapse to their first occurrence
all words anagrams collapse to one
anagram reappears after a break and is kept again

stress.description: many short words over a tiny alphabet so anagram runs occur often
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    words = []
    for _ in range(n):
        ln = rng.randint(1, 4)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice('ab') for _ in range(ln)))
    return [words]
stress.note: two-letter alphabet maximises adjacent anagram collisions
